cards.prompt: Type inference from the argument: Echo(42) infers T=int, Echo("hi") T=string, Echo(3.5) T=double, and typeof(T) yields the reified CLR names, so the output is 42:Int32 hi:String 3.5:Double
cards.code: using System; static string Echo<T>(T x) => $"{x}:{typeof(T).Name}"; Console.WriteLine($"{Echo(42)} {Echo("hi")} {Echo(3.5)}");

cards.prompt: Multi-parameter inference: each type parameter is inferred from its own argument, so Pair(1,"x") gives K=int V=string and Pair(true,2.0) gives K=bool V=double, printing Int32->String | Boolean->Double
cards.code: using System; static string Pair<K,V>(K k, V v) => $"{typeof(K).Name}->{typeof(V).Name}"; Console.WriteLine($"{Pair(1, "x")} | {Pair(true, 2.0)}");

cards.prompt: Overload resolution prefers the more specific non-generic Which(int) for an int argument (prints int) while only the generic Which<T> matches a string (prints generic), so the output is int generic
cards.code: using System; static string Which<T>(T x) => "generic"; static string Which(int x) => "int"; Console.WriteLine($"{Which(5)} {Which("s")}");
